title: chrysobalanus subundulatus
intro: Chrysobalanus subundulatus is a plant species commonly known as the subundulate cuphea or the false garcinia, belonging to the family Chrysobalanaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in West Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chrysobalanus subundulatus include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or decoctions, are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
